what_it_does: Captures a foundational, still-growing design vision for a single unified application that merges the DNA/design, factory/composition, gallery, and company streams into one pre-crystallized system, and states the laws, generative flow, legibility architecture, and face-pipeline inlet that are meant to guide the convergence.
observations: DNA and factory are described as the same application, not yet built, currently split across two streams.
The channel's purpose is to unite all streams into the one system they are becoming.
A layer table assigns roles: DNA carries identity, factory carries types/composition, gallery carries content/scenes, and the company layer carries address resolution and models.
Creation is drawn as a generative flow DNA → factory → gallery, all resolving through the company layer.
The file declares a 'scan before build' law: any plan made without a full-scope scan of sessions, content, and principles will fail.
The interface is framed as Tim's command bridge, not a general-purpose UI.
Legibility architecture says meaning must live in self-describing registry fields, not in the rendering surface.
The whole system is said to be modelled from Tim's seed equation.
A six-stage face pipeline is proposed to translate Claude Design exports into registered company assets.
The decision-card / decision-surface is presented as a reusable primitive for both reconciliation and seed-react design.
standouts: The unifying theorem/seed equation is claimed as the model for the entire system.
Self-describing registry fields are proposed as the universal mechanism for human meaning and progressive disclosure.
The 'scan before build' law makes deep, distributed scanning a hard gate before interface/archetype build.
The face pipeline binds a no-code design tool into the typed registry via extraction, judgment, validation, and reconciliation.
DNA, factory, gallery, and company are treated as areas/handles of one not-yet-built application rather than separate projects.
conventions: Uses date-stamped 'ratified' and 'provisional' markers attributed to Tim or the lead.
Mixes verbatim-in-meaning quotes with lead synthesis and open frame-checks.
Uses wikilink-style RELATES and internal references.
Includes an ASCII generative-flow diagram and a markdown layer table.
Highlights laws and key points with ★ markers.
Ends sections with explicit OPEN questions.
concerns: The layer table is explicitly warned against reifying, yet it is still presented as a structural map.
Many central abstractions (seed equation, decision-surface, registry schemas, address resolution) are named but not defined in this file.
Heavy reliance on ratified principles with no concrete implementation detail or schema shown.
Numerous cross-references to unseen files/concepts make the file hard to understand in isolation.
The boundary between factory types and gallery instances/scenes is admitted as unresolved.
notes: A design-manifesto substrate that tries to hold multiple in-flight streams in one conceptual frame and gives build-gating laws before concrete implementation.
questions: What is the exact seed equation and how does it map to system architecture?
What are the concrete registry schemas and self-describing field requirements?
How is the distributed scan assembled, and who owns the assembled union?
What is the current code-vs-design state in each stream?
What specific mechanism implements the decision-surface and decision-card flow?
What does 'company' address resolution and models refer to in concrete terms?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to be the canonical orientation every stream builds toward; actual content is a growing design manifesto with layers, laws, and a pipeline, which matches that purpose.
apparent_themes: Unification of divergent streams into one pre-built application
Inverted/convergent development rather than top-down construction
Generative design-to-code pipeline with validation and reconciliation
intention_signals: To establish a shared ontology and build-laws so multiple partial streams can converge into a single Tim-directed system, with registries as the source of truth and a design-intent inlet that turns free-form design exports into typed, registered assets.
novelty: Treats several in-flight projects as partial views of a single unbuilt application; locates human meaning in registry self-description rather than UI labels; models the whole system on a seed equation; and proposes a no-code-to-typed-asset pipeline mediated by a reusable decision-card primitive.
contribution: Contributes the unifying conceptual frame, the generative layer model, the scan-before-build law, the legibility-via-registries principle, and the face-pipeline inlet design as a convergence target and design rationale for a fused system.
summary_for_embedding: A growing canonical design document that frames DNA, factory, gallery, and company streams as one unbuilt application, defines a generative identity→types→content flow, mandates scan-before-build, and proposes self-describing registries plus a Claude Design-to-asset face pipeline.
intention_for_embedding: To align all streams around a single system architecture and provide build-gating laws and an inlet for Tim's design intent before implementation crystallizes.
